mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack increases, signaling weakening consumer demand and lower corporate profit growth, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer prices indicates strong aggregate demand outpacing supply, forcing firms to raise margins and boosting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and operational efficiency gains, which compresses input costs and expands profit margins, yielding positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances following an INDPRO acceleration, or the signal's predictive power vanishes outside the post-shock recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1300.0 thousand units, construction activity contracts sharply, reducing demand for materials and financial services, which transmits to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no predictive alpha after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate signals robust job creation without labor market overheating, supporting sustained wage growth and consumer spending that drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative or null Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels increase borrowing costs and erode real purchasing power, prompting central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances that suppress equity valuations and produce negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of high-CPI observations, or the series shows no statistically significant negative correlation with short-term market direction over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue forecasts and investor confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during late-cycle expansion phases.
